shortDescription: For all companies (domestic and foreign) not claiming charitable exemption u/s 11
longDescription: ITR-6 is the income tax return for companies incorporated under the Companies Act 2013 (or earlier). This includes domestic private limited companies, public limited companies, Section 8 companies (not claiming u/s 11), and foreign companies earning income in India. It requires a complete set of financial statements (P&L, Balance Sheet), depreciation schedules, and mandatory MAT computation for most companies.

eligibilityCriteria.detail: Any company registered under the Companies Act, whether private, public, one-person company, or Section 8 company (unless claiming u/s 11).
Foreign companies earning income from India (royalty, FTS, business connection, capital gains on Indian assets) must file ITR-6.
Domestic companies that have opted for 22% (115BAA) or 15% (115BAB new manufacturing) flat rates also file ITR-6.

exclusionCriteria.detail: Section 8 companies registered u/s 12A/12AB and claiming exemption u/s 11 must use ITR-7.
exclusionCriteria.consequence: Must use ITR-7 to claim charitable exemption

exclusionCriteria.detail: Individuals, HUFs, firms, LLPs, AOP/BOI cannot use ITR-6.

commonMisconceptions: Misconception: Small companies don't need to file ITR-6. Reality: ALL companies must file ITR-6 regardless of income level — even loss-making or dormant companies.

Misconception: MAT doesn't apply if company is in losses. Reality: MAT is on book profit, not taxable profit. If book profit is positive (even when taxable income is negative due to carried losses), MAT applies.
Misconception: Companies paying 25% tax don't need MAT. Reality: 25% rate companies must still compute MAT and pay higher of normal tax or MAT (unless opted 115BAA/115BAB).

Misconception: 115BAA and 115BAB are the same. Reality: 115BAA (22%) is for existing companies; 115BAB (15%) is only for new manufacturing companies set up after October 1, 2019 and commencing production by March 31, 2023.
fy202526Changes: Capital gains rates from Finance Act 2024 apply: LTCG on investments at 12.5% (no indexation), STCG via STT at 20%
No change in corporate tax rates — 25% (turnover ≤ ₹400Cr), 30% (others), 22% (115BAA), 15% (115BAB)
CBDT notification requiring detailed disclosure of benami transactions and related party payments in ITR-6
